headerTitle: AI Agent Architect  ·  Production AI Infrastructure  ·  Multi-Agent Orchestration  ·  Enterprise Reliability
subjectText: AI Agent Architect — Edisyl (Remote) — Will Austin
intro: 14 production Claude API endpoints live. Multi-model routing, HITL, MCP protocol — all running against real operational data, not demos. The agent reliability and quasi-determinism problem Edisyl is solving is the specific architecture problem these systems were built around.
bullets: Production AI agent architecture — 14 live endpoints: multi-model routing (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus by task complexity), tool-use architecture, structured JSON output with Zod validation, human-in-the-loop confirm-before-execute, and MCP protocol — all running in production across 13 platforms. Not prototypes. The eval framework comes before the feature, not after.

LLM failure mode literacy — accuracy cliff understood, designed around — built systems that route around model limitations rather than relying on prompting to fix semantic problems. Quasi-determinism achieved through validated tool patterns over raw LLM inference, output consistency checks, and structured fallback routing when agents drift. Zero hallucinations reaching users is the bar.

Agent framework built from first principles — custom orchestration built without defaulting to LangChain abstractions — strong documented opinion on why most agent architectures fail at enterprise scale, and a production system that doesn’t. Context window management, memory strategies, dependency graph design, and failure mode taxonomy built in before deployment.

Observability and eval harness design — structured observability across all 14 production AI endpoints: per-request logging (model ID, prompt version, latency, raw output, correction signals), error handling, output consistency checks, and fallback routing. Non-technical operators trust agent results without checking every output — that is the success condition.
closingLine: The architecture Edisyl is building — agent fleets that produce the same right answer three times in a row on real unclean data — is the exact reliability problem these 14 production endpoints are built to solve. Available immediately. TX-based, remote.
matchNotes: Strong: production multi-agent orchestration, LLM failure mode patterns, custom framework vs LangChain (JD explicitly allows this), eval/observability design, Python+TypeScript. Gaps: 6-10yr Staff-level requirement, no vector DB production, no LangChain/LlamaIndex, no enterprise-scale deployment. Equity upside justifies applying. Requires written application answers inline.
